text: A rich, savory mole made with turkey and local chiles. Less sweet than other varieties, it’s a bold dish served during community gatherings and Tuesday mole traditions.
Crunchy corn tostadas made with lime and salt, topped with nopales, meat, cheese, and spicy salsa. A rustic, satisfying snack that captures the essence of Amealco’s countryside.
Handmade tortillas filled with wild greens, requesón, and poblano strips. Light, earthy, and deeply rooted in Otomí culinary heritage.
